questions.q: What was the palace at Varanavata chiefly built from?
questions.explanation: The house was built of lac, ghee, hemp and other materials chosen to blaze up in moments.

questions.q: In which town was the house of lac built for the Pandavas?
questions.explanation: The lac palace stood at Varanavata, where the Pandavas were sent on the pretext of a festival.

questions.q: Who masterminded the plot to burn the Pandavas in the house of lac?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana, fearing the Pandavas' claim to the throne, conceived the plan to burn them alive at Varanavata.

questions.q: Which agent of Duryodhana built the lac palace?
questions.explanation: Purochana, Duryodhana's trusted minister, built the flammable palace and stayed there to spring the trap.

questions.q: Whose warning saved the Pandavas from the fire at Varanavata?
questions.explanation: Vidura, the Pandavas' wise uncle, warned them of the plot and arranged their means of escape.

questions.q: Who escaped from the burning house of lac?
options: The Pandavas with Kunti and Draupadi
questions.explanation: All five brothers and their mother Kunti escaped; Draupadi was not yet married to them at the time.

questions.q: How did the Pandavas get out of the lac house on the night of the fire?
options: Through a tunnel dug beneath the house
Across the rooftops to the river
Through a postern gate left unbarred
Disguised as mourners in a procession
questions.explanation: A hired miner had dug a secret tunnel beneath the palace, and the family walked out beneath the earth.

questions.q: Which Pandava set the lac house alight before the family fled?
questions.explanation: Bhima fired the trap himself, turning Duryodhana's weapon against his own agent before leading the family out.

questions.q: What did the world believe after the lac house burned?
options: That robbers had fired the town
That the gods had punished Varanavata
That the Pandavas and Kunti had died in the flames
questions.explanation: Five charred bodies and a woman's were found in the ashes, and the world mourned the Pandavas and Kunti as dead.

questions.q: What does the name Lakshagriha mean?
questions.explanation: Laksha means lac, the highly flammable resin from which the deadly palace took its name.

questions.q: How did Vidura warn Yudhishthira of the danger waiting at Varanavata?
options: Through a letter hidden in a gift of fruit
By sending Kunti a dream through a sage
In a riddling speech spoken in a tongue only Yudhishthira knew
By openly denouncing the plan in court
questions.explanation: At the departure Vidura spoke in the Mlechchha dialect, riddling of weapons that are not iron and of escape below the earth.

questions.q: Who sent the skilled miner who dug the escape tunnel under the lac house?
questions.explanation: Vidura quietly dispatched a trusted miner to Varanavata to tunnel a way out beneath the deadly palace.

questions.q: Besides Purochana, who died in the fire and was mistaken for the Pandavas?
options: A Nishada woman and her five sons
Five wandering brahmins and their mother
Five hunters sheltering from a storm
Purochana's five guards and his cook
questions.explanation: A Nishada woman and her five sons, asleep in the house after a feast, burned in the fire and were mourned as Kunti and the Pandavas.

questions.q: How did Hastinapura respond to the news of the fire at Varanavata?
options: Bhishma marched to Varanavata with troops
The court performed funeral rites and mourned the Pandavas
questions.explanation: Believing the Pandavas dead, Dhritarashtra and the court performed the water rites and the city was plunged into mourning.

questions.q: What pretext drew the Pandavas to Varanavata in the first place?
options: A swayamvara of the local princess
A great festival of the god Pashupati held in the town
An invitation to consecrate a new temple
A royal hunt in the surrounding forests
questions.explanation: Courtiers primed by Duryodhana praised Varanavata's festival of Pashupati until the Pandavas agreed to attend, and Dhritarashtra blessed the trip.

questions.q: Why were the Nishada woman and her sons inside the lac house on the night of the fire?
options: They had come to Kunti's almsgiving feast and slept there, heavy with wine
Purochana had hired them to guard the doors
They had taken shelter from a sudden storm
Duryodhana had sent them to spy on the brothers
questions.explanation: Kunti had held a feast of almsgiving, and the woman and her five sons stayed on, sleeping off the wine when the fire was set.

questions.q: Who carried Kunti and his brothers through the forest after the escape from the lac house?
questions.explanation: When the exhausted family could walk no further, Bhima carried his mother and all four brothers through the night forest.

questions.q: Who guided the Pandavas to the town of Ekachakra after the fire at Varanavata?
questions.explanation: The sage Vyasa met the fugitives and directed them to live disguised as brahmins in a Brahmin's house at Ekachakra.

questions.q: How did Yudhishthira first recognize the palace at Varanavata as a trap?
options: A servant of Purochana confessed the plot
He smelled ghee and lac worked into the walls
He found the cellars stacked with oil jars
Birds refused to nest under its eaves
questions.explanation: Already alerted by Vidura, Yudhishthira caught the smell of fat, lac and ghee in the walls and knew the house was built to burn.

questions.q: Who helped the fugitives across the Ganga on the night of the fire?
options: A ferryman who never learned their names
A boatman sent by Vidura with a waiting boat
A bridge of reeds the miner had built
questions.explanation: Vidura had stationed a trusted boatman on the Ganga, who carried the Pandavas and Kunti to the far shore.

questions.q: What became of Purochana, the builder of the lac house?
options: He burned to death in the trap he had built
He was slain by Bhima at the city gate
He fled and was never found
He confessed and was exiled by Dhritarashtra
questions.explanation: Purochana died in his own fire, burned in the palace he had built to murder the Pandavas.

questions.q: By what other Sanskrit name is the house of lac known in the epic?
questions.explanation: Jatu is another word for lac, so the epic also calls the deadly palace the Jatugriha.

questions.q: Who alone grasped the meaning of Vidura's riddling farewell warning about the house of lac?
questions.explanation: Only Yudhishthira understood the coded speech and answered Vidura in kind, later explaining the warning to Kunti.

questions.q: How did the miner prove to the Pandavas that Vidura had truly sent him?
options: He repeated the secret words Vidura had spoken at their departure
He carried Vidura's signet ring as a token
He showed a letter written in Vidura's own hand
He arrived with Vidura's trusted charioteer
questions.explanation: The miner recalled Vidura's riddling warning as his token of trust, and Yudhishthira knew him for a friend.

questions.q: Where did Bhima start the blaze on the night of the escape from the lac house?
options: At the four corners of the compound only
In the kitchens, to look like an accident
At the gates of Varanavata town
At the door where Purochana lay sleeping
questions.explanation: Bhima fired the house first at Purochana's own door, then all around, so the plotter perished in his own trap.

questions.q: Which counsellor had schooled Dhritarashtra in ruthless statecraft shortly before the lac house plot?
questions.explanation: Kanika's cynical counsel on destroying rivals by any means, the Kanika Niti, comes just before the conspiracy in the epic.

questions.q: In which book of the Mahabharata does the burning of the house of lac occur?
questions.explanation: The episode fills its own sub-book within the Adi Parva, the epic's first great book.

questions.q: What development frightened Duryodhana into plotting the fire at Varanavata?
options: Yudhishthira had been named heir apparent and the people loved him
Arjuna had won the alliance of Panchala
Krishna had pledged the Yadava armies to the Pandavas
Bhishma had proposed dividing the kingdom
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra had installed Yudhishthira as heir apparent, and the citizens' open praise of him drove Duryodhana to plot the Pandavas' deaths.

questions.q: How was the mouth of the escape tunnel kept hidden inside the lac house?
options: Behind a false wall in Kunti's chamber
Beneath the hearthstone of the kitchen
Covered with planks set level with the floor
Under a heavy chest that Bhima alone could move
questions.explanation: The miner concealed the tunnel mouth with planks laid flush with the floor in the middle of the house, invisible to Purochana's watchful eyes.

questions.q: Whom did the Pandavas first have to fight after fleeing the burning house of lac?
options: The rakshasa Hidimba in the forest
questions.explanation: Deep in the forest beyond the Ganga the man-eating rakshasa Hidimba sent his sister to take them, and Bhima slew him in the fight that followed.
